steps.title: Identify the growth rate of $f(x) = k(1.84)^x$
steps.content:
When $x$ increases by $1$:

$$f(x+1) = k(1.84)^{x+1} = 1.84 \cdot k(1.84)^x = 1.84 \cdot f(x)$$

So $f$ multiplies by $1.84$ for each unit increase in $x$. This means $p = 84$.

steps.title: Find the exponent structure for g(x) and identify the choice
steps.content:
For $g(x)$ to multiply by $1.84$ when $x$ increases by $4$, the exponent must increase by $1$ when $x$ changes by $4$.

If the exponent is $\frac{x}{4}$, then when $x$ increases by $4$, the exponent increases by $\frac{4}{4} = 1$. ✓

Therefore:
$$g(x) = k \cdot 1.84^{x/4} = k(1.84^x)^{1/4}$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
